-{"id":"bd-gptu","title":"Implement rate-limit governor (adaptive concurrency)","description":"Task: Implement Rate-Limit Governor

Purpose
-------
Dynamically adjust parallelism based on real rate limit data from GitHub
API and model responses, preventing throttling and cascading failures.

Background: Why Adaptive?
-------------------------
Static parallelism is dangerous:
- Too high: hit rate limits, get blocked
- Too low: waste time when limits are fine
- Limits vary by time, account tier, prior usage

Solution: query real limits, adjust dynamically.

Governor State
--------------
GOVERNOR_STATE associative array:
- github_remaining: remaining API calls
- github_reset: when limit resets (epoch)
- model_in_backoff: currently backing off?
- model_backoff_until: when backoff ends
- effective_parallelism: current allowed sessions
- circuit_breaker_open: emergency stop?

Implementation
--------------

start_rate_limit_governor()
  Run in background during review:
  while review lock held:
    update_github_rate_limit
    check_model_rate_limit
    adjust_parallelism
    sleep 30

update_github_rate_limit()
  Query: gh api rate_limit
  Extract: .resources.core.remaining and .reset
  If remaining < 500: log warning, reduce parallelism

check_model_rate_limit()
  Scan session logs for 429/rate limit patterns
  If multiple recent hits:
    Set model_in_backoff = true
    Set backoff_until = now + 60 seconds

adjust_parallelism()
  Start with target = REVIEW_PARALLEL (default 4)
  
  If github_remaining < 1000: target /= 2
  If model_in_backoff: target = 1
  
  Count recent errors in sliding window
  If errors > 5 in 5 minutes:
    Open circuit breaker
    target = 0
    Log emergency pause
  
  Set effective_parallelism = target

can_start_new_session()
  Return false if:
  - circuit_breaker_open
  - model_in_backoff
  - active_sessions >= effective_parallelism
  Otherwise true

Circuit Breaker
---------------
Pattern for handling cascading failures:
- CLOSED: normal operation
- OPEN: too many failures, pause all
- HALF-OPEN: after cooldown, try one (not implemented yet)

Metrics Tracked
---------------
- API calls per minute
- 429 responses per session
- Session error rate
- Effective vs requested parallelism

Integration Points
------------------
- Called before starting new session
- Called in session monitor loop
- Updates TUI status display
- Writes metrics for analytics

Testing
-------
- Mock gh api rate_limit responses
- Simulate 429 responses
- Verify parallelism adjusts
- Verify circuit breaker triggers

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Queries real GitHub rate limits
- [ ] Detects model rate limits from logs
- [ ] Adjusts parallelism dynamically
- [ ] Circuit breaker prevents cascading failures
- [ ] Metrics tracked for analysis","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:38:47.005795803-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:38:47.005795803-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-gptu","depends_on_id":"bd-jm89","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T16:17:00.313011296-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-hmw8","title":"Implement command validation and blocking","description":"Security: Validate commands before execution in agent sessions.

Three categories:

SAFE_BASH_COMMANDS (always allowed):
  git, grep, find, ls, cat, make, npm, cargo, go, python, pytest,
  shellcheck, eslint, prettier, jq, sed, awk, sort, diff

APPROVAL_REQUIRED_COMMANDS (human confirmation):
  rm, mv, cp (file operations)
  curl, wget (network)
  docker, kubectl (containers)

BLOCKED_COMMANDS (never allowed):
  sudo, su, chmod +x, chown, eval, exec

Special gh handling:
  READ allowed: gh issue view, gh pr view, gh issue list
  BLOCKED in Plan mode: gh issue comment, gh issue close, gh pr merge

validate_agent_command() checks command against lists, returns:
  0 = allowed
  1 = blocked
  2 = needs approval

Called by pre-exec hook in agent sessions.

Acceptance: Blocked commands rejected, approval commands queued, gh mutations blocked in Plan mode.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:43:25.152161697-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T17:02:09.080859708-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T17:02:09.080859708-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented command validation with 19 passing tests","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hmw8","depends_on_id":"bd-9s7y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:17.523931567-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-jen3","title":"Implement review command exit codes and error classification","description":"# Task: Implement Review Command Exit Codes

## Purpose
Define and implement consistent exit codes for `ru review` following the ru exit code conventions, enabling automation and scripting.

## Background: ru Exit Code Convention
From AGENTS.md, ru uses these exit codes:
- 0 = Success (all repos synced/reviewed)
- 1 = Partial failure (some repos failed)
- 2 = Conflicts exist (needs resolution)
- 3 = Dependency/system error (gh missing, auth failed)
- 4 = Invalid arguments
- 5 = Interrupted (use --resume)

## Review-Specific Exit Codes

### Exit 0: Success
- All sessions completed successfully
- All questions answered
- All approved changes applied (if --apply)

### Exit 1: Partial Failure
- Some sessions failed but others succeeded
- Some repos could not be reviewed (network, rate limit)
- Partial progress was made

### Exit 2: Conflicts/Review Needed
- Agent detected merge conflicts in worktree
- Quality gates failed (tests/lint)
- Changes require human intervention before apply

### Exit 3: Dependency/System Error
- Claude Code not installed or not in PATH
- gh CLI not authenticated
- tmux not available (for local driver)
- ntm not available (when --mode=ntm specified)

### Exit 4: Invalid Arguments
- Unknown flags
- Invalid --mode value
- Invalid --priority value
- Conflicting options (--plan with --push)

### Exit 5: Interrupted
- SIGINT/SIGTERM received
- --max-runtime exceeded
- --max-questions exceeded
- Checkpoint saved, use --resume to continue

## Implementation

### Error Classification Function
```bash
classify_review_error() {
    local error_type="$1"
    local context="$2"
    
    case "$error_type" in
        session_failed|rate_limited|network_error)
            echo "partial"  # Exit 1
            ;;
        merge_conflict|quality_gate_failed|tests_failed)
            echo "conflict"  # Exit 2
            ;;
        missing_dependency|auth_failed|no_driver)
            echo "system"  # Exit 3
            ;;
        invalid_flag|bad_mode|conflicting_options)
            echo "invalid"  # Exit 4
            ;;
        interrupted|timeout|max_questions)
            echo "interrupted"  # Exit 5
            ;;
        *)
            echo "unknown"
            ;;
    esac
}
```

### Exit Code Aggregation
```bash
aggregate_exit_code() {
    local -a exit_codes=("$@")
    local max_code=0
    
    for code in "${exit_codes[@]}"; do
        [[ $code -gt $max_code ]] && max_code=$code
    done
    
    # Special case: 5 (interrupted) takes precedence over 1 (partial)
    if [[ " ${exit_codes[*]} " =~ " 5 " ]]; then
        echo 5
        return
    fi
    
    echo $max_code
}
```

### Final Exit Handling
```bash
finalize_review_exit() {
    local exit_code="$1"
    
    case "$exit_code" in
        0) log_success "Review completed successfully" ;;
        1) log_warn "Review completed with partial failures" ;;
        2) log_error "Review blocked by conflicts - manual resolution needed" ;;
        3) log_error "Review failed due to system/dependency error" ;;
        4) log_error "Invalid arguments" ;;
        5) log_warn "Review interrupted - use --resume to continue" ;;
    esac
    
    exit "$exit_code"
}
```

## Testing
- Verify each exit code for its conditions
- Verify --resume works after exit 5
- Verify aggregation chooses worst code
- Verify CI can parse exit codes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 6 exit codes defined and documented
- [ ] Error classification works correctly
- [ ] Exit code aggregation from multiple sessions
- [ ] Clear log message for each exit code
- [ ] --resume only available after exit 5

 {"id":"bd-jm89","title":"Define unified session driver interface","description":"# Task: Define Unified Session Driver Interface

## Purpose
Create an abstract interface that both ntm and local drivers implement, enabling graceful degradation and consistent behavior regardless of which driver is used.

## Background: Why a Unified Interface?
- ntm provides advanced orchestration but may not be installed
- Local driver (tmux + stream-json) works everywhere
- Same code should work with either driver
- Enables testing without full ntm dependency
- Future drivers (remote, cloud) can plug in

## Interface Definition

### Driver Capabilities
```bash
# Query what driver can do
driver_capabilities() {
    # Returns JSON with capabilities
    cat << EOF
{
  "name": "local",
  "parallel_sessions": true,
  "activity_detection": false,
  "health_monitoring": false,
  "question_routing": true
}
EOF
}
```

### Core Operations
```bash
# Start a new Claude session
# Args: worktree_path, session_name, initial_prompt
# Returns: session_id
driver_start_session() {
    local wt_path="$1"
    local session_name="$2"
    local prompt="$3"
    # Implementation varies by driver
}

# Send message/answer to session
# Args: session_id, message
# Returns: 0 on success
driver_send_to_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local message="$2"
}

# Get current session state
# Args: session_id
# Returns: JSON with state info
driver_get_session_state() {
    local session_id="$1"
    # Returns: {"state": "generating|waiting|complete|error", ...}
}

# Stop/kill a session
# Args: session_id
driver_stop_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
}

# Interrupt session (Ctrl+C equivalent)
# Args: session_id
driver_interrupt_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
}
```

### Event Streaming
```bash
# Stream events from session
# Args: session_id, callback_function
# Callback receives: event_type, event_data
driver_stream_events() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local callback="$2"
    # Calls: $callback "question" "$question_json"
    # Calls: $callback "complete" "$status"
    # Calls: $callback "error" "$error_msg"
}
```

### Normalized Event Schema
Both drivers emit the same event format:
```json
{
  "type": "init|generating|waiting|complete|error",
  "session_id": "...",
  "timestamp": "2025-01-04T10:30:00Z",
  "wait_info": {
    "reason": "ask_user_question|agent_question_text|external_prompt|unknown",
    "context": "...",
    "options": ["a) ...", "b) ..."],
    "recommended": "a",
    "risk_level": "low|medium|high"
  }
}
```

## Driver Selection
```bash
detect_review_driver() {
    # Try ntm first (preferred)
    if command -v ntm &>/dev/null; then
        if ntm --robot-status &>/dev/null 2>&1; then
            log_verbose "Using ntm driver (robot mode available)"
            echo "ntm"
            return
        fi
        log_verbose "ntm found but robot mode unavailable"
    fi

    # Fall back to local driver
    if command -v tmux &>/dev/null && command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
        log_verbose "Using local driver (tmux + stream-json)"
        echo "local"
        return
    fi

    log_error "No suitable driver available"
    echo "unsupported"
}
```

## Driver Loading
```bash
load_review_driver() {
    local driver="$1"
    
    case "$driver" in
        ntm)
            source "$RU_LIB_DIR/drivers/ntm.sh"
            ;;
        local)
            source "$RU_LIB_DIR/drivers/local.sh"
            ;;
        *)
            log_error "Unknown driver: $driver"
            return 1
            ;;
    esac
    
    REVIEW_DRIVER="$driver"
}
```

## Implementation Notes
- Functions prefixed with driver_ are implemented per-driver
- Common code calls driver_* functions
- Drivers can extend interface with driver-specific features
- Capabilities check enables graceful feature degradation

## Testing
- Mock driver for unit tests
- Verify interface consistency between drivers
- Test driver selection logic
- Verify event normalization

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Interface defined with all required operations
- [ ] Driver selection works correctly
- [ ] Event schema documented and consistent
- [ ] Capabilities query implemented
- [ ] Driver loading mechanism works","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:35:14.284949263-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:39:51.577905888-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:39:51.577905888-05:00","close_reason":"Unified driver interface implemented with: load_review_driver(), driver_capabilities(), driver_start_session(), driver_send_to_session(), driver_get_session_state(), driver_stop_session(), driver_interrupt_session(), driver_stream_events(), driver_list_sessions(), driver_session_alive(). Includes event schema documentation. -{"id":"bd-s3iy","title":"Implement non-interactive mode for CI/automation","description":"# Task: Implement Non-Interactive Mode

## Purpose
Add --non-interactive flag to `ru review` for CI/automation environments where no human is available to answer questions.

## Background
From AGENTS.md:
- Non-interactive mode (--non-interactive) suppresses prompts for CI/automation
- Must work in headless environments (no TTY)

## Non-Interactive Behavior

### Question Handling
When a question arises in non-interactive mode:

1. **AskUserQuestion with recommended option**: Auto-select recommended
2. **AskUserQuestion without recommended**: Log warning, skip item
3. **Agent text question**: Log warning, skip item
4. **External prompt (password, conflict)**: Fail with exit code 3

### Implementation

```bash
handle_question_non_interactive() {
    local question_info="$1"
    local session_id="$2"
    
    local reason recommended
    reason=$(echo "$question_info" | jq -r .reason)
    recommended=$(echo "$question_info" | jq -r .recommended // "")
    
    case "$reason" in
        ask_user_question)
            if [[ -n "$recommended" ]]; then
                log_info "[non-interactive] Auto-selecting: $recommended"
                driver_send_to_session "$session_id" "$recommended"
                return 0
            else
                log_warn "[non-interactive] No recommended option, skipping item"
                record_item_outcome "$repo_id" "issue" "$number" "skipped" \
                    "Skipped in non-interactive mode (no recommended option)"
                driver_send_to_session "$session_id" "skip"
                return 0
            fi
            ;;
        agent_question_text)
            log_warn "[non-interactive] Agent text question, skipping"
            driver_send_to_session "$session_id" "skip"
            return 0
            ;;
        external_prompt)
            log_error "[non-interactive] External prompt requires human - failing"
            return 3
            ;;
    esac
}
```

### Auto-Answer Policy

Configurable via:
```bash
REVIEW_NON_INTERACTIVE_POLICY="auto"  # auto|skip|fail
```

- **auto**: Use recommended if available, skip otherwise
- **skip**: Always skip questions (log them for later)
- **fail**: Fail immediately on any question (strictest)

### Question Logging

In non-interactive mode, log all questions for later review:
```bash
log_skipped_question() {
    local question_info="$1"
    local log_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/skipped-questions-$REVIEW_RUN_ID.json"
    
    echo "$question_info" >> "$log_file"
}
```

### TTY Detection

```bash
check_interactive_capability() {
    # Check if stdin is a terminal
    if [[ \! -t 0 ]]; then
        if [[ "$REVIEW_NON_INTERACTIVE" \!= "true" ]]; then
            log_warn "No TTY detected, enabling --non-interactive mode"
            REVIEW_NON_INTERACTIVE=true
        fi
    fi
}
```

### Summary Output

In non-interactive mode, output a summary of skipped questions:
```
Review completed with 3 skipped questions.
Questions logged to: ~/.local/state/ru/skipped-questions-20250104-103000.json

Use "ru review --resume --interactive" to answer remaining questions.
```

## Flags

```bash
--non-interactive     # Enable non-interactive mode
--auto-answer=POLICY  # Policy: auto (default), skip, fail
```

## Integration Points
- Detect non-interactive at startup
- Handle questions differently based on mode
- Log skipped questions for later
- Provide resume path for answering questions later

## Testing
- Verify TTY detection works
- Verify auto-answer selects recommended
- Verify skip logs questions
- Verify fail mode exits on question
- Verify resume path works

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] --non-interactive flag works
- [ ] TTY auto-detection works
- [ ] Auto-answer uses recommended options
- [ ] Skipped questions logged
- [ ] Clear summary output
- [ ] --resume allows answering skipped questions later

+{"id":"bd-s3iy","title":"Implement non-interactive mode for CI/automation","description":"# Task: Implement Non-Interactive Mode

## Purpose
Add --non-interactive flag to `ru review` for CI/automation environments where no human is available to answer questions.

## Background
From AGENTS.md:
- Non-interactive mode (--non-interactive) suppresses prompts for CI/automation
- Must work in headless environments (no TTY)

## Non-Interactive Behavior

### Question Handling
When a question arises in non-interactive mode:

1. **AskUserQuestion with recommended option**: Auto-select recommended
2. **AskUserQuestion without recommended**: Log warning, skip item
3. **Agent text question**: Log warning, skip item
4. **External prompt (password, conflict)**: Fail with exit code 3

### Implementation

```bash
handle_question_non_interactive() {
    local question_info="$1"
    local session_id="$2"
    
    local reason recommended
    reason=$(echo "$question_info" | jq -r .reason)
    recommended=$(echo "$question_info" | jq -r .recommended // "")
    
    case "$reason" in
        ask_user_question)
            if [[ -n "$recommended" ]]; then
                log_info "[non-interactive] Auto-selecting: $recommended"
                driver_send_to_session "$session_id" "$recommended"
                return 0
            else
                log_warn "[non-interactive] No recommended option, skipping item"
                record_item_outcome "$repo_id" "issue" "$number" "skipped" \
                    "Skipped in non-interactive mode (no recommended option)"
                driver_send_to_session "$session_id" "skip"
                return 0
            fi
            ;;
        agent_question_text)
            log_warn "[non-interactive] Agent text question, skipping"
            driver_send_to_session "$session_id" "skip"
            return 0
            ;;
        external_prompt)
            log_error "[non-interactive] External prompt requires human - failing"
            return 3
            ;;
    esac
}
```

### Auto-Answer Policy

Configurable via:
```bash
REVIEW_NON_INTERACTIVE_POLICY="auto"  # auto|skip|fail
```

- **auto**: Use recommended if available, skip otherwise
- **skip**: Always skip questions (log them for later)
- **fail**: Fail immediately on any question (strictest)

### Question Logging

In non-interactive mode, log all questions for later review:
```bash
log_skipped_question() {
    local question_info="$1"
    local log_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/skipped-questions-$REVIEW_RUN_ID.json"
    
    echo "$question_info" >> "$log_file"
}
```

### TTY Detection

```bash
check_interactive_capability() {
    # Check if stdin is a terminal
    if [[ \! -t 0 ]]; then
        if [[ "$REVIEW_NON_INTERACTIVE" \!= "true" ]]; then
            log_warn "No TTY detected, enabling --non-interactive mode"
            REVIEW_NON_INTERACTIVE=true
        fi
    fi
}
```

### Summary Output

In non-interactive mode, output a summary of skipped questions:
```
Review completed with 3 skipped questions.
Questions logged to: ~/.local/state/ru/skipped-questions-20250104-103000.json

Use "ru review --resume --interactive" to answer remaining questions.
```

## Flags

```bash
--non-interactive     # Enable non-interactive mode
--auto-answer=POLICY  # Policy: auto (default), skip, fail
```

## Integration Points
- Detect non-interactive at startup
- Handle questions differently based on mode
- Log skipped questions for later
- Provide resume path for answering questions later

## Testing
- Verify TTY detection works
- Verify auto-answer selects recommended
- Verify skip logs questions
- Verify fail mode exits on question
- Verify resume path works

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] --non-interactive flag works
- [ ] TTY auto-detection works
- [ ] Auto-answer uses recommended options
- [ ] Skipped questions logged
- [ ] Clear summary output
- [ ] --resume allows answering skipped questions later

 {"id":"bd-sv6v","title":"Implement retry with exponential backoff","description":"Retry with exponential backoff for transient failures.

Function: retry_with_backoff(cmd, max_attempts, base_delay)

Algorithm:
- Start with base_delay (default 1s)
- On failure: delay = base * 2^(attempt-1)
- Add jitter: +/- 25% randomization
- Continue until success or max_attempts

Use cases:
- API calls that timeout
- Rate limit recovery
- Network glitches

Implementation logs warnings on each retry, returns final exit code.

Acceptance: Works for common failure patterns, respects max attempts, jitter prevents thundering herd.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:43:23.01899538-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:43:23.01899538-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-sv6v","depends_on_id":"bd-mnu9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:15.633158875-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-tcns","title":"Design agent prompts for review workflow","description":"Task: Design Agent Prompts for Review Workflow

Purpose
-------
Craft the prompts that instruct Claude on how to review issues/PRs, what
constraints to follow (no gh mutations), and what artifacts to produce.

Background: Prompt Engineering Matters
--------------------------------------
The quality of automated reviews depends heavily on prompt design:
- Clear policy communication (no PRs accepted)
- Explicit constraints (Plan mode = no mutations)
- Required output format (review-plan.json)
- Independent verification emphasis

Prompt Components
-----------------

1. System Context (AGENTS.md Reading)
   First read ALL of AGENTS.md and README.md carefully.
   Use your code investigation agent mode to fully understand the code,
   technical architecture, and purpose of the project. Use ultrathink.

2. Digest Handling
   If prior digest exists: read it, update based on delta commits
   If no digest: create comprehensive fresh digest
   Always write updated digest to .ru/repo-digest.md

3. Policy Communication
   POLICY: We do not accept PRs or outside contributions. The maintainer
   policy is disclosed to users. Claude reviews and independently decides
   whether and how to address submissions. Bug reports are welcome.

4. Review Instructions
   For each work item:
   - Read via gh issue view or gh pr view
   - Verify claims independently - do not trust user reports blindly
   - Check dates against recent commits (many issues may be stale)
   - If actionable: create local commits with fixes/features
   - If unclear: prepare question for maintainer

5. Plan Mode Constraints (CRITICAL)
   - DO NOT run gh issue comment, gh issue close, gh pr comment, etc.
   - DO NOT push any changes
   - Only use gh for READ operations (view, list)
   - All mutations will be applied by ru in a separate phase

6. Required Output
   Must produce .ru/review-plan.json with schema:
   {
     "schema_version": 1,
     "run_id": "...",
     "repo": "owner/repo",
     "items": [...],
     "questions": [...],
     "git": {...},
     "gh_actions": [...]
   }

Prompt Template Function
------------------------
generate_review_prompt() takes:
- wt_path: worktree path
- repo_name: owner/repo format
- work_items: JSON array of items to review
- run_id: unique review run ID

Checks for existing digest and adjusts instructions accordingly.

Prompt Variations
-----------------
- Quick Review: Focus on high-priority items, target 10 minutes
- Deep Review: Comprehensive analysis, architectural implications
- Security-Focused: Security items first, verify all claims

Testing
-------
- Verify Claude produces valid review-plan.json
- Verify gh mutations are NOT attempted
- Verify digest is created/updated
- Verify questions use AskUserQuestion tool

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Prompt template produces working reviews
- [ ] Plan mode constraints enforced
- [ ] Review plan artifact created correctly
- [ ] Policy communicated clearly
- [ ] Digest handling works both cases
- [ ] Questions formatted for aggregation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:37:17.313860817-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:44:47.540328214-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:44:47.540328214-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-tcns","depends_on_id":"bd-koxf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:44:54.883229859-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

+            "${REVIEW_RUN_ID:-unknown}" "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" "$reason" "$escaped" >> "$log_file"
     fi
 }

@@ -0,0 +1,174 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+#
+# Unit tests: Non-interactive review behavior (bd-s3iy)
+#
+# Tests:
+# - log_skipped_question (handles JSON and non-JSON inputs)
+# - handle_question_non_interactive (auto/skip/fail + external_prompt behavior)
+#
+# shellcheck disable=SC1091  # Sourced files checked separately
+# shellcheck disable=SC2317  # Test functions invoked indirectly via run_test
+# shellcheck disable=SC2034  # Globals are referenced by sourced functions
+
+set -uo pipefail
+
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+
+source "$SCRIPT_DIR/test_framework.sh"
+
+source_ru_function "ensure_dir"
+source_ru_function "json_escape"
+source_ru_function "get_skipped_questions_log_file"
+source_ru_function "log_skipped_question"
+source_ru_function "handle_question_non_interactive"
+
+# Mock logging
+log_warn() { :; }
+log_error() { :; }
+log_info() { :; }
+log_verbose() { :; }
+
+# Mock driver send
+SENT_TO_SESSION=""
+driver_send_to_session() {
+    local _session_id="$1"
+    local message="$2"
+    SENT_TO_SESSION="$message"
+    return 0
+}
+
+test_log_skipped_question_wraps_non_json() {
+    local test_name="log_skipped_question: wraps non-JSON input"
+    log_test_start "$test_name"
+
+    local env_root
+    env_root=$(create_test_env)
+
+    export RU_STATE_DIR="$env_root/state/ru"
+    export REVIEW_RUN_ID="run-nonjson"
+
+    log_skipped_question "not json at all" "skip"
+
+    local log_file
+    log_file=$(get_skipped_questions_log_file)
+    assert_file_exists "$log_file" "log file should be created"
+
+    local line
+    line=$(tail -1 "$log_file")
+    assert_equals "skip" "$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.reason')" "reason should be logged"
+    assert_equals "not json at all" "$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.question.raw')" "raw input should be wrapped"
+
+    log_test_pass "$test_name"
+}
+
+test_handle_question_non_interactive_auto_selects_recommended() {
+    local test_name="handle_question_non_interactive: auto selects recommended"
+    log_test_start "$test_name"
+
+    local env_root
+    env_root=$(create_test_env)
+
+    export RU_STATE_DIR="$env_root/state/ru"
+    export REVIEW_RUN_ID="run-auto"
+    REVIEW_NON_INTERACTIVE_POLICY="auto"
+
+    SENT_TO_SESSION=""
+    local question_info
+    question_info=$(jq -n '{reason:"ask_user_question",context:{questions:[{recommended:"Quick fix"}]}}')
+
+    assert_exit_code 0 handle_question_non_interactive "$question_info" "sess-1" "should succeed"
+    assert_equals "Quick fix" "$SENT_TO_SESSION" "should send recommended answer"
+
+    local log_file
+    log_file=$(get_skipped_questions_log_file)
+    assert_file_not_exists "$log_file" "should not log when auto-selecting"
+
+    log_test_pass "$test_name"
+}
+
+test_handle_question_non_interactive_auto_skips_without_recommended() {
+    local test_name="handle_question_non_interactive: auto skips without recommended"
+    log_test_start "$test_name"
+
+    local env_root
+    env_root=$(create_test_env)
+
+    export RU_STATE_DIR="$env_root/state/ru"
+    export REVIEW_RUN_ID="run-auto-skip"
+    REVIEW_NON_INTERACTIVE_POLICY="auto"
+
+    SENT_TO_SESSION=""
+    local question_info
+    question_info=$(jq -n '{reason:"ask_user_question",context:{questions:[{prompt:"x"}]}}')
+
+    assert_exit_code 0 handle_question_non_interactive "$question_info" "sess-2" "should succeed"
+    assert_equals "skip" "$SENT_TO_SESSION" "should send skip when no recommended"
+
+    local log_file
+    log_file=$(get_skipped_questions_log_file)
+    assert_file_exists "$log_file" "log file should exist when skipping"
+    assert_equals "1" "$(wc -l < "$log_file" | tr -d ' ')" "one entry logged"
+
+    log_test_pass "$test_name"
+}
+
+test_handle_question_non_interactive_external_prompt_fails() {
+    local test_name="handle_question_non_interactive: external_prompt fails"
+    log_test_start "$test_name"
+
+    local env_root
+    env_root=$(create_test_env)
+
+    export RU_STATE_DIR="$env_root/state/ru"
+    export REVIEW_RUN_ID="run-external"
+    REVIEW_NON_INTERACTIVE_POLICY="auto"
+
+    SENT_TO_SESSION=""
+    local question_info
+    question_info=$(jq -n '{reason:"external_prompt",context:"Password:"}')
+
+    assert_exit_code 3 handle_question_non_interactive "$question_info" "sess-3" "external prompt should fail"
+    assert_equals "" "$SENT_TO_SESSION" "should not send response"
+
+    local log_file
+    log_file=$(get_skipped_questions_log_file)
+    assert_file_exists "$log_file" "log file should exist on external prompt"
+    assert_equals "external_prompt" "$(tail -1 "$log_file" | jq -r '.reason')" "log reason should be external_prompt"
+
+    log_test_pass "$test_name"
+}
+
+test_handle_question_non_interactive_fail_policy_fails() {
+    local test_name="handle_question_non_interactive: fail policy fails"
+    log_test_start "$test_name"
+
+    local env_root
+    env_root=$(create_test_env)
+
+    export RU_STATE_DIR="$env_root/state/ru"
+    export REVIEW_RUN_ID="run-fail"
+    REVIEW_NON_INTERACTIVE_POLICY="fail"
+
+    SENT_TO_SESSION=""
+    local question_info
+    question_info=$(jq -n '{reason:"agent_question_text",context:"Should I refactor?"}')
+
+    assert_exit_code 3 handle_question_non_interactive "$question_info" "sess-4" "fail policy should fail"
+    assert_equals "" "$SENT_TO_SESSION" "should not send response"
+
+    local log_file
+    log_file=$(get_skipped_questions_log_file)
+    assert_file_exists "$log_file" "log file should exist on fail policy"
+    assert_equals "fail" "$(tail -1 "$log_file" | jq -r '.reason')" "log reason should be fail"
+
+    log_test_pass "$test_name"
+}
+
+run_test test_log_skipped_question_wraps_non_json
+run_test test_handle_question_non_interactive_auto_selects_recommended
+run_test test_handle_question_non_interactive_auto_skips_without_recommended
+run_test test_handle_question_non_interactive_external_prompt_fails
+run_test test_handle_question_non_interactive_fail_policy_fails
+
+print_results
+exit $?
